metric_value: Top 20 IT company revenues suggest spending at or near the trough of current IT recession
notes: Core analytical finding based on Q2 2002 Top 20 IT vendor revenue data

metric_value: IT spending will pick up from current trough; recovery is coming
notes: Aberdeen's directional call that recession is ending

metric_value: IT management must recognize key directional changes as industry emerges from recession
notes: Prescriptive warning: ignoring changes leads to missed opportunities

metric_value: Out-of-step IT suppliers will lose market share when spending recovers
notes: Suppliers who don't recognize structural IT management changes will be displaced

metric_value: EAI (Enterprise Application Integration) is a key technology priority for post-recession IT spending
notes: Identified in Aberdeen keyword list as a spending priority technology

metric_value: Security is a key technology priority for post-recession IT spending
notes: Identified as a structural shift in IT management priorities

metric_value: CRM is a key technology priority for post-recession IT spending
notes: Identified in Aberdeen keyword list as a spending priority

metric_value: Organizations must recognize structural IT management changes to avoid missed opportunities
notes: Framework factor: awareness of change is prerequisite for capturing recovery upside

metric_value: Recovery timing is critical; early movers on structural changes will capture market share
notes: Framework factor: timing of response to structural changes affects competitive outcome

metric_value: IT spending grew 9% in 2003 (after approximately flat 2002 and -8% 2001); recovery materialized
notes: Per CNET/Gartner July 2002 data; recovery arrived in 2003 confirming Aberdeen's trough assessment

metric_value: IT security became one of fastest-growing IT spending categories from 2003 onward
notes: Aberdeen's identification of security as a key priority proved correct; security spending grew consistently post-2003

metric_value: CRM software market recovered strongly from 2003; Salesforce.com led cloud CRM boom
notes: Aberdeen's identification of CRM as a recovery priority proved correct; Salesforce went public in 2004

metric_value: 2002 represents trough year for IT recession that began in 2001 following dot-com bust
notes: Context for the analysis: Q2 2002 data was the data source for the trough assessment
